steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger. It connects to أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent) to emphasize that the sending definitely happened.
Added آ at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent) to show who did the sending.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning to show causing someone to do something. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) in this pattern.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word comes after إِنَّآ (indeed we) which emphasizes the statement.

steps.m: Noah (the prophet sent to his people)
steps.n: Name of the prophet Noah who was sent as a messenger to warn his people
The tanween shows Noah is the object of أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent) in this ayah
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'Nuh' to 'his people', showing he was sent to them.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to stand/rise up (basic meaning that evolved to mean 'people' as those who stand together)
steps.n: Root meaning evolves to mean 'people/folk' as a noun
Root letters ق-و-م got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the word 'people' - like a group standing together
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِ with extension marks (ۦٓ) to show possession by him. This word follows إِلَىٰ (to) in the phrase 'to his people'
This is a connecting word that joins أَنذِرْ (warn) with what comes after it. It works like 'to' in English phrases like 'told him to go'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding أَ at start, changing middle vowel to ِ, and adding sukun at end. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to warn his people.
Root meaning evolves to mean 'people/folk' as a noun
Connected letters with vowel marks to form a noun meaning a group that stands together
Added كَ at the end to show these are 'your' people. This word receives the action of the command 'warn' (أَنذِرْ)
Basic preposition showing starting point or origin. Here it combines with قَبْلِ to mean 'from before'
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word connects to what comes after it
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form meaning 'before'

steps.m: before (connected to أَن يَأْتِيَهُمْ - that it comes to them)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it, forming the phrase 'before that it comes to them'
Basic particle that connects to the verb أَنذِرْ (warn) to show what was sent to Noah - the command to warn his people
Basic present tense form
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, with vowels to complete the word. This verb describes what عَذَابٌ (punishment) does.

steps.m: comes to them (punishment comes to them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who receives the action. The fatha (َ) connects it to the word. This shows the punishment will come to them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: a punishment (subject of يَأْتِيَهُمْ - comes to them)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'punishment'. The tanween (ٌ) shows it's indefinite (like 'a/an' in English). This word is the subject doing the action of 'coming to them' in the verse.
Becomes an adjective meaning painful
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed to intensive adjective pattern showing strong quality
Added double dammah to make it indefinite

steps.m: he said (introducing his statement يٰقَوْمِ إِنِّى لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This word introduces what he said next: 'O my people, indeed I am to you a clear warner'
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا, which is very common for hollow verbs

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/gathering together becomes a word for a group of people who gather as one. This is the basic word before showing who it belongs to.
Adding kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: O my people! (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: The يٰ at the start is used to call out to someone directly. This whole phrase is what is being said in قَالَ (he said).
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Added ي (with a shadda on the ن) at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This word connects to قَوْمِ (my people) who are being addressed.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (being warned by نَذِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. In this ayah, it shows who is being warned by the warner (نَذِيرٌ).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he warned) to a noun meaning 'warner/one who warns' by following the فَعِيل pattern
The tanween (double-damma) shows this is indefinite (any warner). This word is describing what the speaker is, connected to the earlier word إِنِّى (indeed I am).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he made clear' to 'one who makes clear' by adding mu- at start

steps.m: one who makes very clear (describing نَذِيرٌ)
steps.n: The shadda adds emphasis. This word describes نَذِيرٌ (warner) in the ayah.

steps.m: one who makes very clear (describing نَذِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is describing the warner (نَذِيرٌ)
This is a basic connecting word that introduces what comes next, like saying 'that' in English. In this ayah, it connects to the command 'worship' (ٱعْبُدُوا۟) that follows it.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to connect it smoothly to the command word that follows it (ٱعْبُدُوا۟ - worship).
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this is about something that happened in the past
Changed from 'he worshipped' to a command 'worship!' by adding U sound at start and putting sukoon at end
Changed to address multiple people by adding وا at the end and changing the last vowel. The alif at start changed to hamzat-wasl (ٱ)
Changed the concept of worship into a noun meaning 'a deity'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is being worshipped in أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ال to specify THE one God, doubled the ل with shadda to emphasize His uniqueness, and added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of worship in this ayah
Adding vowels to make basic past tense verb form
Changed from 'he protected' to 'protect yourself!' by adding ٱتَّ at the start to make it a command to do the action to yourself
Changed from commanding one person to commanding many people to protect themselves
Added conjunction 'and'

steps.m: and be mindful of Him! (command to group)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'him' to show who to be mindful of
Connected root letters with vowels to form past tense verb

steps.m: he made (someone) obey / he caused obedience
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Changed middle of word to make it passive voice
Added damma at end to show it's present tense
Adding 'and' (و) at the start to connect this command with the previous one
Adding و makes this a command for a group rather than one person
Added object pronoun نِ (me) to show who should be obeyed

steps.m: to forgive, pardon, cover up faults

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and ending with damma

steps.m: he will forgive (as a result of following Allah's command)
steps.n: Changed the final damma to sukun because this verb comes after a command, showing it's a result of following that command
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added ك with damma to show this is addressing multiple people

steps.m: for you all (object of يَغْفِرْ - He will forgive)
steps.n: Added م to complete the plural 'you'. This word shows who will receive Allah's forgiveness in the phrase 'He will forgive for you all'

This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects 'forgive' (يَغْفِرْ) with 'your sins' (ذُنُوبِكُمْ), showing which sins will be forgiven.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a sukun (ْ) in يَغْفِرْ. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters together to make the basic noun for a single sin
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural, showing many sins
Added kasra to connect it to the next part
Added كُمْ to show the sins belong to you all (plural)

steps.m: to delay or put something after its usual time

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense verb form with shadda, meaning 'he delayed'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed final vowel to sukun because it comes before another word
Added كُمْ to show who is being delayed
Added وَ at beginning to connect this action with the previous one (يَغْفِرْ - he forgives)
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to أَجَلٍ (appointed term) to show 'to an appointed term'
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long 'aa' sound
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show the sound is stretched slightly when reading
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun

steps.m: a term/deadline (being acted upon by إِلَىٰٓ - to)
steps.n: The word gets a special ending mark (tanween kasra) because it comes after the word 'to' (إِلَىٰٓ)

steps.m: to name or make something high/elevated

steps.n: Added مُ at start to show something receiving the action of naming. The middle letter has a shadda (doubled) to show it was actively named by someone.
Adding tanween (double fatha) makes it indefinite - meaning 'a' specified time rather than 'the' specified time

steps.m: indeed (emphasizes the statement about أَجَلَ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. It's like adding 'truly' or 'definitely' before a statement in English.

steps.m: indeed, certainly (emphasizes the statement about أَجَلَ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'

steps.m: an appointed term (object of إِلَىٰٓ - towards)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to form the noun. This word is the object that إِلَىٰٓ (towards) points to in the phrase 'towards an appointed term'.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the first lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and replaced alif with wasl-alif since it comes after another word. The shadda on lam shows emphasis.
This is a time word that connects two events. Here it connects something coming/arriving (جَآءَ) with what happens after. Like saying 'when X happens, then Y happens'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Added alif after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
This is the basic negative particle
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to delay or put something back

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing it's not just a simple delay but an intensive or complete delay
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start, and made passive by adding damma at end - means 'being delayed' instead of 'delayed'
Basic form of the word that introduces an impossible or unlikely condition. Different from إِنْ which is used for possible conditions.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ل and sukun on و
Added damma and sukun to connect the letters. The middle weak letter و drops out in this form.
Added تُ at the end to change from a command (be!) to past tense meaning 'I was'
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by adding م at the end to show it's talking to a group
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes 'he knew' to 'you know' by adding تَ at start and changing vowels to show present tense
Changes from singular to plural subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense. This word shows who is speaking to his Lord in this ayah.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter and removing the last vowel creates a noun for someone who does this action completely
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like saying 'truly' or 'definitely' at the start of a sentence.
Added ى at the end to show this is about me (I). This connects to the next word دَعَوْتُ (I called) where I am doing the action.

steps.m: to call out to someone, to invite

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: I called out (to قَوْمِى my people)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action. This word connects to قَوْمِى (my people) who were being called.

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Basic noun form showing a group of people
Added ى to show these are 'my' people. This whole phrase قَوْمِى is what's being acted on by the verb دَعَوْتُ (I called).
Added the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) and 'sukoon' (ْ) to the root letters to make the word mean 'night'

steps.m: at night (time when دَعَوْتُ/I called happened)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes when Noah called his people. This connects to the verb دَعَوْتُ (I called) in the ayah, telling us when he did the calling.
Adds alif after second root letter to turn the concept of flowing light into the noun 'day'
Adding ًا to the end makes it mean 'during the day' - it tells us when something happened
Adds و to connect this with لَيْلًا (by night) showing the calling happened both day and night
Starting with the first particle فَ which shows sequence or result
Adding لَ which adds emphasis to what follows (like saying 'surely' or 'indeed')

steps.m: and then not (negates يَزِدْهُمْ - increase them)
steps.n: Adding مْ which makes the following verb negative in the past. This particle combination negates the verb يَزِدْهُمْ (increase them) in this ayah.

steps.m: to make more or add to something
steps.n: The middle letter is a weak letter (ي)
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make past tense 'he' form of verb meaning 'to add/increase'
Changed from past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel

steps.m: he makes more of them/adds to them (object of فَلَمْ)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Changed the word pattern to make it a noun meaning 'the act of calling/praying'. The first vowel changes to damma, and we add ء at the end.
Added ي at the end to show it's 'my prayer'. The آ is just a longer way of writing ا, and ِ helps connect it to ي. The ٓ mark shows the ي is pronounced long.
This is an exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before
Added alif at the end to make the word complete. Shows that something is being left out or excluded - in this case, it means 'except' when talking about their response to the invitation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the vowel on first letter to kasra and added alif after second letter to make it a noun showing the action itself

steps.m: running away (as the thing being increased in يَزِدْهُمْ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is the object of يَزِدْ (increase). The sentence means 'my calling only increased them in running away'
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Added ى at the end to show this is about me. This connects to the verb دَعَوْتُ (I called) in the next part of the verse.
Added damma (ُ) to first letter and doubled the middle letter to show completeness
Added fatha (َ) to prepare for connecting with ما

steps.m: whenever (used with دَعَوْتُهُمْ - I called them)
steps.n: Added ما to make it mean 'whenever'. This word connects to دَعَوْتُهُمْ (I called them) to show 'whenever I called them'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end and changing the last letter from ا to و

steps.m: I called them (referring to those who Noah was inviting to faith)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who was being called. In this ayah, Noah is talking about calling his people to believe in Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something else was done
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb تَغْفِرَ (you forgive) to show who is being forgiven.
Added هُمْ to show this refers to multiple people (them). This word shows who is receiving the forgiveness in تَغْفِرَ (you forgive).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added masculine plural past tense suffix وا to show multiple people did this action. This word is part of a series of actions they did when being called to faith.
Added elongation mark (ـٓ) after وا to show proper pronunciation when continuing to next word
Adds إِ at start to make the singular noun meaning 'one finger'
Changes from singular 'finger' to plural 'fingers' by rearranging letters and vowels in a special pattern
Changed to accusative case since it's the object of جَعَلُوا (they put/placed)
Added pronoun to show possession. This word is the object of جَعَلُوا (they put/placed) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the fingers are being placed inside their ears.
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to show the extended pronunciation of the ى. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
The word becomes a noun meaning the body part used for hearing
Changes from singular 'ear' to dual 'two ears' and adds a sound at the end to connect to next word
Adds هِمْ at the end to show these are their ears
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Form X adds seeking/requesting meaning to the base verb
Added و (and) at start to connect words + changed ending to وا to show 'they' did the action
Changed from the root meaning 'to clothe' into a simple noun meaning 'a piece of clothing' by adding vowels
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple garments

steps.m: their clothes (object of وَٱسْتَغْشَوْا۟ - they covered)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show the clothes belong to them. This word is the object of the verb وَٱسْتَغْشَوْا۟ (they covered) in this ayah.
Added shadda to show strong persistence
Adding أَ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added وَ to connect this word to what came before it
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show someone actively seeking or trying to make themselves greater than they should
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous actions in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: he tried to make himself seem greater
steps.n: Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show someone is trying to make themselves something

steps.m: the act of trying to make oneself seem greater
steps.n: Changed from a verb (he tried to be great) to a noun (the act of trying to be great)

steps.m: arrogance (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence. It's describing how they acted - with arrogance.
This is a common connecting word that links events in order. The shadda (doubled م) makes the word stronger.
Added damma (ُ) to show how to pronounce the first letter

steps.m: then (connecting to إِنِّى دَعَوْتُهُمْ جِهَارًا - 'Indeed, I invited them publicly')
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to emphasize the connection between events. This word connects to the next phrase, showing what happened after previous events.
This is a special word that makes statements stronger. The shadda (ّ) shows how strong the emphasis is.

steps.m: indeed I (subject of دَعَوْتُهُمْ - I called them)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show 'I' am doing the action. This connects to the next word دَعَوْتُهُمْ (I called them) where I am doing the calling.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. The ا changes back to وْ when we add endings.
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was called - 'them'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show it's a noun form
Adding ا makes it stronger - like the difference between 'open' and 'wide open'
Added tanween alif to show this describes how something was done
Basic form of the connecting word that shows sequence

steps.m: then (connects to إِنِّىٓ أَعْلَنتُ - indeed I announced)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and fatha (َ) with shadda (ّ) to complete the word. This word connects to what comes after it, showing that 'announcing' happened after the previous action.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to statements. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' at the start of what you're saying.

steps.m: indeed I (connected to أَعْلَنتُ - I announced)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show 'I' am doing the action. This connects to the next word أَعْلَنتُ (I announced) to show who is speaking.

steps.m: indeed I (connected to أَعْلَنتُ - I announced)
steps.n: Changed the writing style of ي to ىٓ which doesn't change the meaning - it's just a different way of writing the same sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to make something public/open' rather than just 'being open'. Like turning a private thing into a public announcement.
The تُ at the end means 'I' did the action
This shows who something is being done to or for

steps.m: to/for them (referring to the people being addressed in أَعْلَنتُ - I announced)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which refers back to the people being addressed in the previous word 'I announced'. This connects the two words to show who is receiving the action.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: to cause to be secret, to conceal
steps.n: Added أ at the beginning to show 'making something happen'. The middle vowels change to match this pattern.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action

steps.m: and I kept it secret (connected to أَعْلَنتُ - I announced)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this with the previous word أَعْلَنتُ (I announced) - showing contrast between announcing and keeping secret
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition
Added هُ (with damma) to show this refers to a single male

steps.m: to/for them (referring to the people being addressed in أَعْلَنتُ)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change the pronoun from singular to plural. This shows the preposition refers to multiple people being addressed in the verb أَعْلَنتُ (I announced).
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that means the act of hiding
Added tanween alif to show this describes how something was done, like saying 'he spoke secretly'
The middle letter و drops out and is replaced with a sukun because it's a hollow verb. The first letter gets damma to show it's a command.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past

steps.m: and then I said (followed by ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action came after something else. This word is followed by ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ (seek forgiveness) to show what was said.

steps.m: to cover up or remove faults

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Adding 'است' at the beginning changes the meaning to seeking or requesting the action
The ُ at the end shows this is happening now (present tense)

steps.m: seek forgiveness! (all of you) (command to the people in فَقُلْتُ)
steps.n: Changed from 'I seek' to 'all of you seek!' by removing أ from start and adding وا at end to make it a command for many people

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow (lord)
steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the caring quality
Added كُمْ at the end to show this lord belongs to all of you. This word is the object of اسْتَغْفِرُوا (seek forgiveness) in this ayah.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: indeed he/it (referring to رَبَّكُمْ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added هُ (he/it) to show who the word is talking about. Here it refers back to رَبَّكُمْ (your Lord) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he was (describing how Allah is غَفَّارًا/forgiving)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to forgive or cover up mistakes

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The doubled letter ف with shadda (ّ) and the ا after it show this is talking about someone who forgives again and again. This is one of Allah's names, showing He constantly forgives.

steps.m: constantly forgiving (as a description being used)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being used as a description in the sentence, like saying 'He was forgiving' rather than just stating 'forgiving'

steps.m: to send or dispatch something from one place/person to another

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يُ at the start to show it's happening now (present), and changed middle vowels to match present tense pattern
Changed the final vowel to kasra because this word is connected to عَلَيْكُم (upon you) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed و to ا and added ء to make it a noun meaning 'the elevated thing' (sky)
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: the sky (object of يُرْسِلِ - He sends)
steps.n: Changed اَل to ٱل (different way of writing), lengthened the ا to آ for emphasis, and added َ at the end to show it's the object receiving the action of 'sending'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: upon you all (receiving the rain from السَّمَآءَ)
steps.n: The suffix كُم shows this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the people who will receive the rain (السَّمَآءَ) that Allah sends.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif after second root letter and changed first vowel to kasra to show continuous action
Added مِ at start to make it mean 'thing that does the pouring'
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after the word السماء, making it flow better when read together

steps.m: abundantly (describing how السماء/the sky/rain is sent)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this word is describing how the action happens. It's describing how Allah sends the sky/rain (يُرْسِلِ ٱلسَّمَآءَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to 'i' sound
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with previous statements

steps.m: and He will increase you all (with أَمْوٰلٍ wealth)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who receives the action. This connects to the next word أَمْوٰلٍ (wealth) which shows what will be increased.
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال (māl) by adding the vowel marks - a common pattern for nouns
Changes from singular 'māl' to plural 'amwāl' - a common plural pattern for nouns
Adds genitive case ending to show this word is being acted upon by something
The alif (ا) changed to a long alif (ٰ), while keeping the tanwin kasra ending

steps.m: with wealth (connected to يُمْدِدْكُم - He will extend/aid you)
steps.n: Added ب prefix meaning 'with'. This shows how Allah will aid them - with wealth.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun meaning 'sons'
The word changed to show it's receiving the action 'provide' - like saying 'sons' became 'with sons'. The ن at the end shows this.

steps.m: and sons (multiple, acted upon by يُمْدِدْ)
steps.n: Adding و at the start means 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this happens in present/future. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) and final letter gets damma (ُ)

steps.m: and he makes (for لَّكُمْ - for you)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start. Final damma is dropped because this connects to what comes after it. This verb is doing something for لَّكُمْ (for you) in this ayah.

steps.m: surely for/to (connected to يَجْعَل - he makes)
steps.n: Adding shadda (ّ) emphasizes the meaning, making it stronger like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: surely for you all (connected to يَجْعَل - he makes)
steps.n: The word 'for' (لِ) gets 'you all' (كُمْ) added to its end to mean 'for you all'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Adding ة at the end turns the concept of covering into a noun for a place that provides cover - a garden
Changed from singular to plural by replacing ة with ات

steps.m: gardens (as the object being made for you)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra to show this is the object of يَجْعَل (he makes/establishes)
This is the basic meaning showing the action of making or putting something
Adding fathas connects the letters and shows this happened in the past
Adding ي at the start and sukun on the middle letter shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding و connects this to the previous statement about giving wealth and children. This word is followed by لَّكُمْ showing He makes these things specifically for you.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is being given or directed to someone. Here it's connected to the verb يَجْعَل (he makes/places)
The shadda (double letter) adds emphasis to the preposition

steps.m: surely for you all (connected to يَجْعَل)
steps.n: The كُمْ at the end means 'you all' (when talking to a group of men or mixed group)
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make the basic noun form
Added أ at start and ا after ه to make it plural. This is a common way to make plurals in Arabic.
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يَجْعَل (makes/places). Like saying 'Allah makes rivers' where 'rivers' is what's being made.
This is the basic form of the question word 'what'

steps.m: what (is wrong with you) - expressing surprise or criticism
steps.n: The shadda (doubled م) adds emphasis to show strong questioning. In this ayah, it's asking 'what is wrong with you?' in a way that shows Allah is questioning why people aren't showing proper respect to Him.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition ل

steps.m: for you all/to you all (addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added the second person masculine plural pronoun كم with damma and sukun to show it's being addressed to multiple people
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَرْجُونَ (you hope) into 'you do not hope'. It's one of the most basic negative words in Arabic, like saying 'no' or 'not' in English.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
ت shows it's either 'you' (male) or 'she' doing the action

steps.m: you (all) hope/expect (for اللَّهِ وَقَارًا - respect for Allah)
steps.n: Added نَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb connects to 'respect for Allah' which comes after it in the ayah.
Present tense verb for 'you all', made by adding تَ at start and ون at end. The و is pronounced but نَ shows it's plural 'you'
This is the unique name of God in Arabic. The word comes from the root meaning 'divine' but has special spelling and pronunciation rules.

steps.m: for/to Allah (connected to تَرْجُونَ - you hope)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show the meaning 'for/to'. The ِ at the end shows this word is affected by another word. In this ayah, it's connected to تَرْجُونَ (you hope), showing what they don't hope for.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to create an abstract noun meaning 'dignity' or 'majesty'

steps.m: dignity/majesty (object of تَرْجُونَ - you hope for)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this word is receiving the action in the sentence. It is what is being hoped for in the phrase 'Why do you not hope from Allah for dignity?'

steps.m: definitely (emphasizes that خَلَقَكُمْ 'He created you' has happened)
steps.n: This is a particle that emphasizes that an action has definitely happened in the past. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah has certainly created humans.

steps.m: And definitely (emphasizes that خَلَقَكُمْ 'He created you' has happened)
steps.n: The و connects this phrase to what came before it, adding the meaning of 'and'
Added fathas to connect the root letters together, making it a past tense verb

steps.m: he created you all (object of قَدْ before it)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who was created. This word comes after قَدْ which emphasizes that this creation definitely happened.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'a stage'
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and changing middle vowels - makes one stage into many stages

steps.m: stages, phases (as the object being acted upon in خَلَقَكُمْ - He created you)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this word is the object of the verb خَلَقَكُمْ (He created you)
This letter turns a statement into a question that expects the answer 'yes'
Adding لَ makes the question stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'really' in English

steps.m: Have (you) not...? (expecting 'yes, we have' as answer)
steps.n: Adding مْ makes the verb that follows negative. Together with أ, it asks 'Haven't you?' expecting 'Yes, we have' as the answer

steps.m: to see with eyes or mind

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed to present tense
Added وْا to show multiple people are being addressed. The word is part of a question 'Have you not seen?' with أَلَمْ before it.

steps.m: how (asking about the way something is done)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about manner, state, or condition. In this ayah, it's asking about how Allah created the heavens.
Adding the vowels makes it a complete question word that can be used in sentences
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is connected to سَبْعَ سَمٰوٰتٍ (seven heavens) which tells us what Allah created.
Changed the concept of worship into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who created seven heavens)
steps.n: The word for 'god' became 'Allah' - the One True God. The ال (al) merged into the word in a special way, making the L sound stronger. The U at the end shows Allah is doing the action.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper noun. The fatha goes on the first letter, sukun on the middle letter.
This word is what Allah created (the object), so it gets a fatha at the end to show it's being acted upon.

steps.m: to rise up, be elevated, or be high

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high above us
Changes to feminine plural form by adding وات. The و appears because the root has و as its last letter.
Adds genitive case ending (kasratan) to show this word is the object of a preposition
The alif (ا) is written as a small line above (ٰ) in this common spelling of 'heavens', with the -in ending showing it receives the action

steps.m: to stack or layer one thing upon another

steps.n: Forms a plural noun showing multiple things arranged in layers

steps.m: in layers (describing how سَمٰوٰتٍ/heavens are arranged)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to make it describe how the heavens are arranged in layers, one on top of another

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, Allah is making the moon into a light and the sun into a lamp.

steps.m: and he made (something into something else)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this with the previous statement about creation. This shows Allah did multiple acts of creation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic noun

steps.m: the moon (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the beginning and fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb جَعَلَ (He made)
Basic preposition meaning 'in' - refers to being inside the seven heavens mentioned earlier in the Quran

steps.m: in them (referring to the seven heavens)
steps.n: Added هِنَّ to show this refers to multiple feminine things (the heavens) mentioned before
Added damma on first letter and connected letters to make the noun 'light'

steps.m: a light (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is 'a light' and is being acted upon. Allah made (جَعَلَ) the moon a light.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something Allah did in the past. This word is part of describing how Allah created the universe.

steps.m: and He made/put (referring to Allah's creation of الْقَمَرَ - the moon)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous action, showing this is part of a sequence of things Allah created. This word specifically refers to how Allah placed the moon (الْقَمَرَ) in the heavens.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together - fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and shadda on ش because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object that Allah made into a lamp (سِرَاجًا)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'lamp'. This word is what Allah made the sun to be.
Added ًا at the end to show this is describing what the sun was made into - a lamp that gives light.
This is the base divine name 'Allah' - a unique proper noun for God that doesn't come from a regular root
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of أَنۢبَتَكُم - grew you)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show that Allah is the subject (doer) of the action in the next word (growing/bringing forth)

steps.m: And Allah (as the doer of أَنۢبَتَكُم - grew you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the verb causative - showing that someone/something is making the growth happen. In this ayah, Allah is causing humans to grow.

steps.m: He caused you all to grow (Allah caused you to grow from الْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: Adding كُم at the end shows who is being grown - you all. This connects to Allah (He) causing humans to grow from the earth.
This is a preposition showing where something comes from or originates. In this ayah, it shows that Allah made you grow from the earth.
The shadda (doubled letter) adds emphasis to the م
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word (الْأَرْضِ - the earth) and adds the meaning of 'the'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a basic noun
Added ٱلْ to make it specific - 'the earth' instead of just any earth
The kasra at the end shows this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah. When مِنَ comes before a word, that word gets a kasra ending.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to grow' to noun 'growth'. This word is what Allah caused to grow from the earth.
The ًا at the end shows this describes how Allah made you grow - as a growth/plant from the earth

steps.m: then (connects to يُعِيدُكُمْ - He will return you)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to the next action يُعِيدُكُمْ (He will return you).

steps.m: then (connects to يُعِيدُكُمْ - He will return you)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) on ث and ّ (shadda) on م make this word stronger and more emphatic, showing a clear break or pause between events
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to Form IV pattern to show causing something to happen
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing internal vowels

steps.m: he will return you all (into the earth)
steps.n: Added plural 'you' as object of the verb. This connects to فِيهَا (in it) which refers to the earth mentioned earlier.
Basic preposition showing location or position

steps.m: in it/her (referring to الأَرْضِ 'the earth' which is feminine)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this refers back to الأَرْضِ (the earth) which was mentioned earlier. Since 'earth' is feminine in Arabic, we use the feminine 'it/her'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to Form IV pattern which makes the meaning causative - making something happen instead of just happening

steps.m: he makes you all come out
steps.n: Attached pronoun suffix meaning 'you all' (masculine plural) as direct object

steps.m: and he makes you all come out
steps.n: Adds conjunction prefix meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to exit' into noun meaning 'act of making exit'

steps.m: a bringing out (object of يُخْرِجُكُمْ - He brings you out)
steps.n: Added tanween (double line ending) to show this is the direct object - it receives the action of 'bringing out' from يُخْرِجُكُمْ (He brings you out)
This is a unique proper name that doesn't follow normal word patterns. It's derived from الإله (the god) but has special status as God's personal name.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in جَعَلَ (made/placed)

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it

steps.m: to make or set something in a particular way
he made (connected to لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ - for you the earth)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, Allah is telling us He made the earth spread out for us.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition

steps.m: for you all (connects لَكُمُ to جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to جَعَلَ (He made) to show what was made 'for you all'

steps.m: earth, land, or ground (something wide and spread out)

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make it mean 'the earth' or 'land' as a specific place
Added 'the' at the beginning. This word is what Allah made (جَعَلَ) in this verse.

steps.m: the earth (as the object that Allah made)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon - Allah made the earth into something

steps.m: to spread or extend something flat

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun for the thing that is spread out. The kasra at the start and alif after س show this is a noun for the object.
Adds tanween alif to show this is the object that Allah made the earth into. This word is the second object of جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: so that you all may follow a path
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - 'in order to/so that'

steps.m: so that you all may follow a path (from مِنْهَا)
steps.n: Added shadda (double letter) on ل and a small mark at the end to show how to pronounce it correctly
This is a preposition showing something comes from somewhere. In this ayah, it shows paths coming from somewhere.
ها refers back to Earth (which is feminine in Arabic). The ayah is talking about paths that you can take from the Earth.
Added damma vowels to create the plural form meaning multiple paths

steps.m: paths (object of لِتَسْلُكُوا - for you to travel upon)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action - these are the paths that you will travel upon
Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make a noun meaning 'wide path'
Makes the word plural by adding an alif between the second and third root letters
Added tanwin alif to show this word is describing the roads (سُبُلًا) mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it past tense meaning 'he said'
Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a root
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show this is the subject doing the action of saying
Added tanween (ٌ) to show this is a complete independent noun at the start of the statement

steps.m: to nurture, care for, or be in charge of
Lord (one who nurtures and cares for all)
steps.n: The doubled letter ب shows this is someone who does a lot of nurturing and caring
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this means 'my Lord'

steps.m: O my Lord! (addressing Allah in Nuh's speech)
steps.n: The shadda on the ر shows Nuh is calling out to Allah. This word is part of what Nuh said when speaking about his people.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being talked about - in this case, it refers to the people who disobeyed Prophet Nuh

steps.m: to disobey or rebel against authority

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he disobeyed'
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding و at the end to show multiple people
Added نِى at the end to show that I am the one being disobeyed. In this ayah, this is Nuh (Noah) speaking about how his people disobeyed him.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif with kasra at start and shadda on ت to show intentional following
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect with previous statement
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
This is a relative pronoun that introduces a clause describing which people Nuh is talking about. It connects to the verb يَزِدْهُ (increased him) that comes after it.
This is the basic negative particle that makes verbs past tense and negative, like changing 'he writes' to 'he did not write'
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the word, making the meaning stronger
Adding يَ at start makes it 'he does' in present time
The ي is dropped and a sukun is added when لَمْ comes before the word, making it mean 'did not increase'
Added هُ to show who is being increased. This word comes after لَمْ (not) in the ayah, so it means 'did not increase him'
Added fatha after first letter and changed و to ا to make the basic noun form. This is a common pattern for hollow roots (roots with و in middle).
Added damma at the end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence. It's the subject of يَزِدْ (increase).
Added هُۥ at the end to show the wealth belongs to him. This whole word is doing the action of not increasing in the verse.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'child'
The هُۥٓ means 'his'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَزِدْ (increase). Along with مَالُهُۥ, it shows what did not increase except in loss.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to مَالُهُۥ (his wealth) as they are both subjects of يَزِدْ (increase)
This is a basic exception particle that shows something is different from what came before. It's like saying 'but not this thing' in English.
The shadda and alif are added to complete the particle. In this ayah, it connects what came before to the word خَسَارًا (loss), showing that the increase mentioned earlier only led to loss.

steps.m: to lose or decrease in value/worth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning 'loss'
Added tanween to make it indefinite accusative

steps.m: a loss (as the object being described in this ayah)
steps.n: Makes this word the object being described, showing what kind of increase was happening - it was an increase in loss

steps.m: he plotted (followed by مَكْرًا كُبَّارًا - a mighty plot)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form. This word is followed by مَكْرًا كُبَّارًا which describes their plot as mighty/great.

steps.m: they plotted (followed by مَكْرًا كُبَّارًا - a mighty plot)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added وَ (and) at the beginning
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'plot'. This is what was plotted in the verse.

steps.m: a mighty plot (object of مَكَرُوا - they plotted)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what was plotted. This connects to the next word كُبَّارًا (mighty) to mean 'a mighty plot'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to make it stronger, adding shadda and alif to show intensity
Added tanween alif to show this describes how big their plot was. This matches with مَكْرًا (plot) which it's describing.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا when we add the fathas. This is a common change in Arabic.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he said' to 'they said'. The ُ (damma) comes before وا to help connect the sounds.
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and'. This connects it to what comes next in the ayah where they say 'Do not leave your gods.'
Basic negative particle that tells us the action after it will not happen
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding ت at start instead of و, and adding ُ at end to make it present tense

steps.m: you all must not leave (emphatic prohibition)
steps.n: Added نَّ for emphasis and prohibition. This appears in a negative command context (لا تَذَرُنَّ) making it a strong prohibition.
Changes from verb 'to worship' to noun meaning 'a god'
Changed from singular 'god' to plural 'gods' by adding letters at start and end
Added fatha mark to show this word is the object in the sentence
Added suffix meaning 'your' when speaking to multiple people
The hamza and alif merged into one long ā sound (آ) - the word means 'your gods'
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous phrase. This connects to the verb تَذَرُنَّ (leave/abandon) to say 'and do not leave/abandon'
Adding ت at the start makes it a present tense verb for 'you'
Added ت with fatha at start to show 'you' as the doer. The و from root drops because it's a weak letter.
Added a 'u' sound (damma) at the end because it's a present tense verb not connected to anything after it
Added نَّ at the end to add strong emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Turned into a proper noun - name of a deity
Adding shadda makes it a specific name rather than general 'love'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being talked about in the sentence, connected to لَا تَذَرُنَّ (do not leave)
Added fatha to show this is the negative particle لَا that negates the verb تَذَرُنَّ (leave/abandon) in this ayah
Added وَ at the start to connect this with the previous negative command, showing a list of things they should not leave/abandon
Turned into a proper noun - name of a deity
The pattern فُعَال makes it a proper noun with emphasis
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb تَذَرُنَّ (do not leave/abandon) in the ayah
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. Here it's negating يَغُوثَ (Yaghuth) in a list of false deities
The و connects this negation to the previous items in the list of things being negated

steps.m: Yaghuth (object of تَذَرُنَّ - do not leave)
steps.n: This is a proper noun representing the name of an idol worshipped in pre-Islamic Arabia. In this verse, it's the object (thing being acted upon) of the verb تَذَرُنَّ (do not leave/abandon).

steps.m: Yaghuth (object of تَذَرُنَّ - do not leave)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تَذَرُنَّ (do not leave/abandon)

steps.m: Ya'uq (an idol's name that was worshipped alongside وَدّ, سُوَاع, and يَغُوث)
steps.n: This is a proper noun for one of the five major idols mentioned in this verse that were worshipped by the people of Prophet Nuh. Since it's a proper name, it doesn't come from an Arabic root.

steps.m: and Ya'uq (connected to يَغُوث before it)
steps.n: The و connects this idol's name to يَغُوث which came before it in the list of idols being mentioned
This is the basic meaning of the root letters
Basic noun pattern transforms root into noun meaning eagle/vulture. This is one of the idols that was worshipped.
The ًا ending shows this is a direct object (something being acted upon) - in this case an idol they worshipped
The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this with what came before it

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that أَضَلُّوا has definitely happened)
steps.n: This is a common particle that emphasizes past actions or certainty

steps.m: and indeed/certainly (emphasizes that أَضَلُّوا has definitely happened)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This و means 'and'.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show basic past tense meaning

steps.m: he made (someone) lose the right path
steps.n: Form IV pattern makes the meaning causative - 'to make someone go astray'. The word كَثِيرًا is the object (in accusative case) showing who was led astray - 'many people'.

steps.m: they made (many) lose the right path (acted on كَثِيرًا)
steps.n: The وا shows this is they (masculine plural) doing the action
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective meaning 'much' or 'many'. This describes how many people were led astray.
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of leading astray - they led many people astray
This is the basic negative particle
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative phrase with what came before. This particle negates the verb تَزِدِ (increase) that comes after it.
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added kasra at the end to show a specific type of command
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who wrongs others (object of تَزِدِ - increase)
steps.n: Changed from 'he wronged' to 'wrongdoer' - like changing from the action to the person who does it. It's being used as the object that's receiving the action of 'increase'
The ين ending makes it plural (more than two). This word is being acted upon by the verb تَزِدِ (increase), showing what should not be increased.
Added ٱل (the) at the start and َ at the end. The ل combines with ظ to make it doubled (ظّ).
Basic form of the exception particle
Added shadda on ل and alif at end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it's excluding the word ضَلٰلًا (misguidance) from what came before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: the act of wandering off (object of يَزِدِ)
steps.n: This changes the verb (wandering) into a noun (the act of wandering). The word is receiving the action of يَزِدِ (increase) in this ayah.

steps.m: wandering off (as the thing being increased)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is what's being increased in the sentence
Combined the two basic particles مِن (from) and مَا (what) together
The ن from مِن merges into the م of مَا, shown by the shadda mark (ّ) which means we double that letter when speaking
Root letters got vowels to make the word mean 'a mistake' instead of 'to make a mistake'
Adding ي changes it from 'mistake' to 'something/someone very mistaken'
Added feminine ending to create specific instance noun
Changed to plural form
Added possessive pronoun 'their'
This is the basic meaning showing what happens when something goes under water
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning to show someone is causing the drowning to happen to others
Changed the vowels to show this happened to them rather than them doing it
Added وا at the end to show this happened to many people
These are the three basic letters that give us the concept of entering
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added أ at start to show making someone else do the action
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا at the end to show this happened to a group

steps.m: and so they were made to enter
steps.n: Added فَ at start to connect this with what happened before - they were drowned, and so they were made to enter the fire
The و (weak letter) changes to ا and we add fatha on the first letter to make it a noun meaning 'fire'

steps.m: a fire (object of أُدْخِلُوا - they were made to enter)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this is indefinite (a fire) and is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's what they were made to enter into.
Starting with the basic particle combination. This word is made up of two particles: ف (and/so) + لم (not in past)
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation. This word negates the verb يَجِدُوا۟ (they found) that comes after it in the ayah.
Basic past tense form showing someone did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. The word is negated by لَمْ which comes before it in the ayah.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُم at the end to show this is 'for them' (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Basic form of the word that shows movement away from something or comparison between things
The letter م has a shadda because it comes after a word ending in ن or م, causing them to blend together when pronounced. It still means 'from' but shows connection to the previous word.
Used to indicate something lesser or besides something else
Added damma on first letter and connected letters to make a word meaning 'below' or 'besides'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'Allah' (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, showing it means 'besides Allah'
Added emphasis by doubling the ل letter
Changed to show 'of' or 'belonging to' by adding the small line (kasra) under the last letter
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
This pattern shows someone who helps or supports others. The alif after the first letter and kasra under the second letter create the 'doer' pattern.
This is a broken plural form showing multiple helpers/supporters. The hamza at the start and the pattern change makes it plural.

steps.m: helpers/supporters (as the object being searched for)
steps.n: The tanwin shows this is indefinite and accusative. This word is the direct object (what was not found) in the phrase 'they did not find helpers'.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense. This is the basic form before the weak letter changes.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter. This word refers to what Noah (نُوحٌ) said.
Adding conjunction و to connect with previous statement
Connected the letters and added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject doing the action of saying (قَالَ)
Added tanween (double damma ٌ) to show this is a complete standalone name
The root letters ر-ب-ب become رَّبّ (Lord) with a shadda, and adding يا before it (though not written) makes it mean 'O Lord!' - a way to call out to Allah

steps.m: O my Lord! (Nuh speaking to Allah)
steps.n: The kasra shows possession - this is Nuh addressing his Lord. This connects to قَالَ نُوحٌ (Nuh said) before it.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'. In this ayah, it's used with تَذَرْ (leave) to say 'do not leave'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to leave something behind or abandon it

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he left' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by: 1) Adding تَ at start to show 'you', 2) Dropping the weak letter و, 3) Adding ُ at end to show present tense
Changed to command form by replacing the ُ with ْ at the end
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing position above something. Here it shows position relative to 'the earth' (الْأَرْضِ)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Added ال to make it specific and ِ because it comes after the word عَلَى (on/upon) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word الْكٰفِرِينَ (the disbelievers). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he rejected' to 'someone who rejects' - like changing 'ran' to 'runner'

steps.m: those who reject truth (acted upon by مِنَ)
steps.n: Added plural ending ين because it's describing multiple people. The ين ending also shows it's affected by the word مِنَ (from) before it.
Added ٱلْ to specify these particular disbelievers. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called a dagger alif.

steps.m: to dwell, stay in a place

steps.n: Added shadda (double letter) to show someone who does a lot of dwelling/staying in places - like a frequent dweller
The و changed to ي because it's easier to pronounce

steps.m: a single dweller (object of تَذَرْ - do not leave)
steps.n: Added tanween (double fatha) to show this is what is being acted upon by the verb تَذَرْ (do not leave)
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain and important

steps.m: indeed you (subject of تَذَرْ - you leave)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is talking about 'you'. This connects to the next word تَذَرْ (you leave) where 'you' is doing the action.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects two parts: 'if you leave them' leads to 'they will misguide your servants'
The initial و is dropped and تَ is added to show 'you' (masculine singular) in the present tense. This is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah.

steps.m: you leave them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: The هُمْ suffix means 'them' (masculine plural), referring to the disbelievers that were mentioned earlier in the surah. The ْ (sukun) is added before هُمْ to help with pronunciation.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added أ at beginning and doubled the last letter to show causing someone else to stray
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to worship' into plural noun meaning 'worshippers'

steps.m: Your servants (object of يُضِلُّوا/they lead astray)
steps.n: Adds possessive pronoun suffix. This word is the object (the one receiving the action) of the verb يُضِلُّوا which means 'they lead astray'.
This is a negative particle that makes things not happen
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative statement to what came before it
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. The initial و drops because it's a weak letter.
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This appears in a negative context (لا) in the verse, so it means 'they will not give birth'
This is the base form of the exceptive particle that excludes what follows from what came before

Added shadda and alif to complete the particle. In this ayah, it excludes 'wicked disbelievers' from 'Your servants', showing that if left alone, these people will only produce offspring who are wicked disbelievers.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it describes what type of children the disbelievers will produce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The intensive form فَعَّال indicates someone who does the action frequently or intensely

steps.m: a constant rejecter of truth (describing the wicked person فَاجِرًا)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this describes the wicked person (فَاجِرًا) mentioned earlier in the verse
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures in a complete way
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: forgive! (a request to Allah in this prayer)
steps.n: Changed to command form by adding alif at start, putting sukun at end, and changing middle vowel to kasra. This is used here in a respectful request to Allah.

steps.m: forgive! (a request to Allah in this prayer)
steps.n: Changed alif to hamzat al-wasl to help pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, just helps with reading.
This is a preposition that shows who will receive the forgiveness

steps.m: for me/to me (object of اغْفِرْ - forgive)
steps.n: Added ى to show this means 'me'. This word is receiving the action of forgiveness in the phrase 'forgive me'
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action (like changing 'parent' to 'one who parents')
Added لِ at beginning to show direction or purpose
Added fatha to prepare for possession marker
Added يَّ (ayya) at the end to mean 'my' and show it's both parents
Changed وَا to وٰ for writing style, and ي to ى for style

steps.m: whoever (refers to people who will enter)
steps.n: This is a word that refers to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is happening 'for' or 'to' someone

steps.m: and for whoever (enters my house as a believer)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous request in the prayer

steps.m: he entered (action before بَيْتِىَ my house)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a completed action. This word shows who entered 'my house' (بَيْتِىَ) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun for a physical building or dwelling place
Added ِى at the end to show the house belongs to me. The َ at the end shows this house is being acted upon by the verb دَخَلَ (entered) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he believed' to 'believer' by adding مُ at start and making middle letter ؤ

steps.m: a believer (as object of دَخَلَ - entered)
steps.n: The word gets an -an ending to show it's receiving the action of 'entered' - like saying 'entered as a believer'
Added مُ at start and hamza in middle to change from 'to believe' into 'one who believes'
Added ين to make the word plural (more than one believer)
Added لِلْ (for the) which combines لِ (for) with ال (the)
Added the conjunction وَ (and) to connect this phrase with what precedes it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to show the person who does the action of believing
Added feminine plural ending to show this refers to multiple women who believe
Added وَ (and) at the start plus ال (the) to make it 'and the believing women', connecting it to words before it

steps.m: and the believing women (acted upon by forgiveness)
steps.n: Gets a kasra (i) ending because it's being acted upon by the word 'forgive' in the prayer 'My Lord forgive... the believing women'
This is a negative particle that stops something from happening. Here it's telling Allah not to increase something.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the prayer. This connects to the previous requests to Allah.

steps.m: to increase or add to something

steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. The ي is dropped because it's a weak letter in this form.
Added kasra at the end to show this is a command or request
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past-tense verb
Changes from 'he did wrong' (verb) to 'wrongdoer' (noun) by adding alif after first letter. Like changing 'steal' to 'thief'. This wrongdoer is being acted upon in the sentence.
Changed from singular 'wrongdoer' to plural 'wrongdoers' by adding ين at the end

steps.m: the wrongdoers (acted upon by تَزِدِ - increase)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَزِدِ (increase). The ل in ٱل causes the ظ to be doubled.

steps.m: except/but (excludes تَبَارًۢا from the previous statement)
steps.n: This is a special word that excludes what comes after it (تَبَارًۢا - destruction) from what came before (the prayer for forgiveness). It's like saying 'Give everyone good things... but not the wrongdoers'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (destroyed) to a noun (destruction) to name the action itself
The ًا ending shows this is what's being affected by the verb تزد (increase) - so the full meaning is 'do not increase them except in total destruction'
